column1: # OT Fail-Safe Position Documentation Template
# Description: Documents fail-safe positions for crown jewel assets.
#   Essential for the Safety Assessment phase (Phase 3) of OT
#   incident response. Must be pre-populated by OT Engineering
#   before an incident occurs.
# Usage: Complete one row per crown jewel asset (Tier 1 and Tier 2
#   as minimum). OT Engineering provides technical details; SOC
#   validates accessibility during incident response.
# Maintenance: Review quarterly or when asset configuration changes.
#   OT Engineering owns accuracy; SOC consumes during incidents.
# Related: Chapter_11_IR_Playbook_Template.md (Phase 3)
#          Chapter_11_5_Post_Containment_Validation.md
# Schema Fields:
# AssetHostname        : string  — Asset hostname from OT_AssetRegister
# AssetIP              : string  — Primary IP address
# CrownJewelTier       : int     — 1 (Safety)
# PurdueLevel          : string  — Purdue level (L0
# DeviceType           : string  — PLC
# ProcessFunction      : string  — Brief description of what this device controls
# FailSafe_NetworkLoss : string  — Behaviour if network connectivity is lost
# FailSafe_PowerLoss   : string  — Behaviour if power is removed
# FailSafe_Reset       : string  — Behaviour if reset to factory/last-known state
# BackupAvailable      : string  — Yes/No — is there a redundant/backup system?
# BackupAsset          : string  — Hostname/IP of backup system (if applicable)
# ManualControlOption  : string  — Can process be controlled manually? Description.
# MaxTimeWithout       : string  — Maximum time process can safely run without this asset
# GoldenImageLocation  : string  — Path/reference to validated configuration backup
# LastValidated        : date    — Date the fail-safe positions were last verified
# ValidatedBy          : string  — OT Engineer who last validated
# Notes                : string  — Additional context for incident responders
# Sample CSV:
AssetHostname
SIS-BURNER-001
column7: Holds last safe state; trips to safe shutdown if watchdog expires
column8: De-energise to trip - SAFE (fail-safe by design)
column9: Returns to safe state - all outputs de-energised
column12: Manual emergency stop buttons at furnace
column13: 0 minutes - SIS must always be operational
column17: SIS is architecturally independent of BPCS network

column1: PLC-PUMP-003
column7: Holds last commanded state - CAUTION if pump was running at high speed
column8: Pump stops - process overheating risk within 8 minutes
column9: Reloads default program - pump starts in safe low-speed mode
column12: Local start/stop at MCC panel; no speed control
column17: Standby pump requires manual switchover at MCC

column1: HMI-CR-001
column7: Displays freeze on last values - operators lose live view
column9: Reboots to login screen - 3-4 minutes to restore
column12: Local gauges available for critical parameters only
column13: 15 minutes before operator intervention likely needed
column17: Backup HMI covers 80% of primary HMI screens

column1: SCADA-SVR-001
column6: Primary SCADA server - all plant polling
column7: PLCs continue running but no supervisory control or trending
column8: PLCs continue running; polling resumes on restart
column12: PLCs run independently; operators use local HMIs
column17: Hot standby automatic failover tested monthly

column1: EWS-050
column7: No impact on running process; cannot program PLCs
